name: Patrick Michael Tobey
citizenship: U.S. Citizen – Eligible for Security Clearance

items.title: Valencia College
bullets: Bachelor of Applied Science, Computer Technology & Software Development

items.title: Missouri Western State University
bullets: B.S., Computer Science (transferred prior to completion)

items.title: Leidos
bullets: Engineered a sophisticated input data scoping system (React/TypeScript) for automatic, by-value data transfer between components, rapidly adapting it within one day to meet revised specifications after a requirements miscommunication, earning praise for accurately fulfilling complex functionality.

Contributed to optimizing the RSPack-based build system by refining its integration and developing a comprehensive demonstration that successfully showcased a full Webpack-to-RSPack replacement for a core project area.

Developed custom backend API endpoints and defined new data types (leveraging .NET C# and a virtualized database architecture) to deliver core functionalities for a monthly project status review application and a dynamic component input scoping system.

Led migration of a critical HR sync from SOAP to REST (PowerShell, C#) after the vendor sunsetted SOAP support, completing ahead of schedule; inherited a broken integration, stabilized it, and received high praise from HR event teams upon delivery.
Became the primary owner and leader of the Cvent Active Directory sync, maintaining and improving the integration that keeps employee records current across enterprise systems.
Remediating Log4j vulnerabilities across legacy Java integration API repositories and contributing to the planning of their broader modernization effort.

On loan to the EHS team to help deliver a critical API integration; identified an opportunity to leverage the main team's internal API hosting platform as a solution, turning the engagement into a win-win for both teams.
Building a reusable Node.js SDK to bridge the EHS integration with the main team's API platform, while guiding a colleague building a parallel Python SDK.

Designed and built from scratch a deterministic AI output system with hallucination markers that provide verifiable signals for whether AI-generated content was fabricated — entirely original architecture, currently in active development.
Developed a Python proof-of-concept demonstrating how OData can be used to enable AI to query structured databases, shared with upper management as part of broader AI capability exploration.
Serve as the team's local AI expert, advising the tech lead on AI architecture decisions and bringing hands-on MCP experience to internal AI tooling and integration workflows.
Built and maintain custom MCP servers locally, applying hands-on MCP architecture expertise to internal AI tooling decisions at Leidos.

items.title: Leidos
bullets: Spearheaded year-long research into Module Federation for a microfrontend architecture, diagnosing Webpack compatibility issues; subsequently identified and championed RSPack as a solution, developing demos that led to its adoption, significantly faster build times, and a streamlined development workflow.

Conceptualized and designed a novel database virtualization system for flexible, user-creatable 'virtual' tables within a rigid SQL structure; this design was subsequently adopted and fully implemented by project leadership using EF Core.

Contributed a large share of development to a major project progress visualization portal, including: engineering backend logic for status review entries; defining new data types for tracking monthly project progress using a self-designed virtualized table system; and developing 12 key data visualizations.

items.title: Missouri Western State University
bullets: Led a team of three students that designed and developed a website and database for biological specimens found during a school biology research project.
Presented the website and database at the 2022 Conference of Applied Learning in Higher Education.

items.title: Freelance
bullets: Commissioned for $500 to design and develop a Discord bot in Java that interfaced with Wynncraft's API to track player looting patterns across server worlds, helping users identify unlooted worlds for high-value item (mythic) farming.
Designed and developed additional plugins for Minecraft servers in Java.

items.title: local-memory-mcp
items.sub_title: Python, ChromaDB, MCP, Docker, SSE, sentence-transformers
bullets: Designed and shipped a local-first personal RAG memory system for AI assistants, exposing MCP tools (store, search, update, delete, version chains) over both stdio and SSE transports.
Implemented versioned update chains with soft-delete, deprecation flags, and conflict logging so memory history is preserved rather than destructively overwritten.
Built heuristic reconciliation and warning-first write responses with structured self-heal hints, enabling AI agents to self-correct risky writes without human intervention.
Architected around an AIX (AI eXperience) philosophy — prioritizing text-first chunks with minimal metadata over rigid schemas, optimizing for practical LLM retrieval quality.
Shipped full Docker Compose support, optional bearer/OAuth auth modes, health checks, and backup/restore — production-ready for self-hosted personal and team deployments.
Released as MIT open source with 4 stars; includes full documentation covering setup, architecture, integrations (ChatGPT + Claude Desktop), and a public roadmap.

items.title: Food Oasis
bullets: Served as tech lead on a 5-person team, taking point on architecture and technical decisions given my experience level relative to teammates.

Built a web application to combat food deserts in the Orlando area, allowing users to search for healthy recipes with allergy/dietary filters, view nearby grocery stores on an interactive map, and discover local farmers markets.
Gained practical experience leading a collaborative team project from design through implementation across a full TypeScript/JS stack.

items.title: Devoria
bullets: Founded and lead development of Devoria, a Minecraft MMORPG built in Java on the Paper server framework.
Designed and implemented a custom item and damage system using custom item stats.
Designed and developed a custom mob system using Model Engine.

items.title: Library Management System
bullets: Built a Java library management system with a GUI frontend and Java backend as a school project.

items.title: Wynncraft Command Block Content
bullets: Built command block puzzles and cutscenes for the 1.19 Silent Expanse update.
Fixed command block-related bugs and collaborated with Grian on a community video task.

items.title: Scouts BSA

bullets: Achieved Eagle Scout rank with 5 Eagle Palms.
Led over 60 scouts as Senior Patrol Leader.
Brotherhood Member of the Order of the Arrow (Scouts BSA honor society).
Participated in approximately 100 hours of community service.

items.position: Club President, Vice President, and Member
bullets: Directed club meetings and registered club teams into tournaments.
Participated in and led 3 hours of community service.
